title: corrigiola littoralis subsp. telephiifolia
intro: Corrigiola littoralis subsp. telephiifolia, also known as the pink catchfly or rock catchfly, is a flowering plant that belongs to the madder family (Madderaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Corrigiola littoralis subsp. telephiifolia has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
